description: The first-person singular pronoun: the speaker as subject, object, possessor, or prepositional object. It carries no gender, rank, or case distinction. Syntactic position supplies the role.
articulatory_notes: Nasal voice begins behind closed lips and opens onto high-front 'i'. A separate unstressed 'a' follows stressed 'mi', and the hiatus remains audible.
usage_notes: A bare clause-initial 'mia' is the subject. Possession uses ordinary noun order or the verb 'phelu'; plural reference is 'lo mia'.
